verificationNote: Requirements constructed from IEC 62443, NIST SP 800-82, and NACSA Act 854 obligations. Not endorsed by any standards body — verify against authoritative sources before use in formal assessments.
name: Configuration Management
description: OT configuration management ensures that changes to OT system configurations are authorised, tested, and tracked. Configuration drift — unauthorised changes accumulating over time — is a common finding in OT security assessments and can mask malicious changes.

requirements.name: OT baseline configuration and hardening
requirements.description: Establish and maintain a security baseline configuration for each OT system type. Baseline follows vendor hardening guides and removes unnecessary services, default credentials, and unused features.

technical.summary: Vendor hardening guides form the basis of OT baseline configurations. Common hardening actions: disable telnet, change default passwords, disable unused ports, remove unnecessary Windows services.
actions: Obtain vendor hardening guides for all major OT components (Siemens, Rockwell, Honeywell, ABB publish hardening guides)
Create baseline configuration checklist per OT system type (HMI, engineering workstation, SCADA server, historian)
Apply hardening to all new OT systems before commissioning
Assess existing OT systems against hardening baseline — identify and remediate gaps on risk-prioritised schedule
Use ICS-CERT/CISA hardening guides as supplementary reference for common platforms (Windows-based OT systems)

governance.summary: Baseline configurations reviewed annually and updated when vendor guidance changes.
actions: Annual baseline review
Baseline update triggers reassessment of deployed systems
Hardening compliance report produced annually

evidenceItems: Baseline configuration documents per system type
Hardening assessment records per OT asset

slMapping.sl1: Some hardening applied; vendor defaults changed
slMapping.sl2: Documented baseline per system type; hardening applied to new systems; existing systems assessed
slMapping.sl3: Automated configuration compliance checking; deviation alerts; hardening applied to all in-scope systems
slMapping.sl4: Cryptographically verified configurations; immutable config for safety systems; hardware secure boot

requirements.name: Change management for OT
requirements.description: All changes to OT system configurations, programs, and network settings must follow a formal change management process with technical review, operational approval, and rollback planning.

technical.summary: OT change management process: change request, technical review (OT engineer), operational approval (operations manager), scheduled maintenance window, rollback plan, post-change verification.
actions: All OT configuration changes (PLC programs, HMI changes, network device config, firewall rules) require formal change request
Emergency change process defined for safety-critical situations — approval still required but expedited
Rollback plan documented for every change before change window begins
Post-change functional verification performed and recorded
Change log maintained linking change request to implementation record

governance.summary: Change management owned by OT engineering. OT manager approves significant changes. Safety team involved for any change to safety-related systems.
actions: Monthly change review meeting
Emergency change retrospective review within 5 days
Annual change process effectiveness review

slMapping.sl2: Formal change management; rollback plan; post-change verification
slMapping.sl3: Automated change detection; drift alerts; all changes in version-controlled repository
slMapping.sl4: Dual-authorisation for safety system changes; safety impact assessment required; formal SIL re-verification

requirements.name: Configuration monitoring and drift detection
requirements.description: Detect unauthorised or undocumented changes to OT system configurations in near-real-time — distinguishing between authorised (through change management) and unauthorised changes.

technical.summary: Compare current OT configurations against approved baseline. Alert on deviations. Distinguish authorised changes (linked to approved change request) from unauthorised drift.
actions: Use OT monitoring platform (Dragos, Claroty, Nozomi) to detect PLC program changes via passive observation
Deploy file integrity monitoring (Tripwire, AIDE) on OT Windows-based systems
Configure alerts for configuration changes not linked to approved change requests
Reconcile configuration monitoring alerts with change management log weekly
Any unreconciled configuration change treated as a potential security incident

governance.summary: Configuration monitoring alerts reviewed daily. Unreconciled changes escalated to OT security team.
actions: Daily alert review
Weekly reconciliation report
Unreconciled changes trigger security investigation

evidenceItems: Security investigation records for unreconciled changes

slMapping.sl2: File integrity monitoring on OT workstations; manual reconciliation
slMapping.sl3: Automated configuration change detection; passive PLC program change detection; real-time alerts
slMapping.sl4: Cryptographic configuration verification; any deviation triggers immediate incident response
